name: Members First Credit Union of N.H.
description_short: Members First Credit Union of New Hampshire is a member-owned financial institution offering checking, savings, loans, and digital banking services to NH residents and communities.
description_long: Members First Credit Union of New Hampshire is a credit union headquartered in New Hampshire that serves individuals and families in the state. As a member-owned cooperative financial institution, it operates under the credit union model rather than as a traditional for-profit bank. The organization maintains physical branch locations across New Hampshire and provides digital banking access to members. The credit union offers a comprehensive range of consumer banking products and services. These include checking and savings accounts, vehicle loans, personal loans, home equity loans and lines of credit, IRAs, and debit card services. Members can access their accounts through mobile banking with features like mobile deposit, online banking portals, and a newly launched NextGen digital banking platform described as modern and customizable. The institution also provides fraud protection services and customer support through multiple channels including phone, email, branch visits, and online contact forms. Members First distinguishes itself through community involvement and member focus. The organization runs an "Illuminating Change" program that supports local nonprofits, with a Q2 2026 spotlight partner being the Manchester Police Athletic League. The credit union emphasizes personalized service, having introduced a digital assistant character named "Ember" to guide members through banking needs. Their NextGen Banking Experience represents an investment in modernizing digital services for tech-forward members. As a credit union, Members First operates on a not-for-profit basis returning benefits to members rather than shareholders, which typically results in competitive rates and lower fees. The institution appears well-established and committed to both digital innovation and community engagement, making it suitable for New Hampshire residents seeking member-centric banking.
services: Home equity loans and lines of credit
Customer service via phone, email, and online contact
pros: Credit union structure means profits return to members rather than external shareholders
Multiple account access channels including mobile app with mobile deposit capability
NextGen digital banking platform described as modern, intuitive, and customizable
Offers home equity loans and lines of credit for debt consolidation and home improvements
Community-focused with Illuminating Change nonprofit partnership program
Multiple contact options including phone (800-860-3832), email, branches, and online forms
Vehicle and personal loan applications available for both existing members and non-members
cons: Limited to New Hampshire members only, restricting access for out-of-state customers
Website does not display specific interest rates, APRs, or fee schedules for comparison
Mobile deposit temporarily unavailable during scheduled maintenance windows
No information provided about minimum account balances, membership eligibility requirements, or account opening fees
Limited details on IRA offerings, CD rates, or other savings product specifics
best_for: New Hampshire residents seeking member-owned banking with community focus
Borrowers looking for home equity loans or personal loans from a local credit union
Customers who value digital banking innovation and customizable banking platforms
Community-minded savers wanting their financial institution to support local nonprofits
diagnosis: Members First Credit Union is best for New Hampshire residents seeking traditional banking services with a community focus and modern digital capabilities. The main caveat is that it is geographically restricted to NH members and the website lacks detailed rate and fee information needed for competitive comparison with other regional credit unions and banks.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Members First Credit Union of New Hampshire is a member-owned financial institution offering checking, savings, and loans.
